purpose: Record chapter-level companion-note decisions for reader, e-reader, and audio review without moving meaning-critical caveats out of the reader spine.

records.title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
records.reader_treatment: Keep proof receipt states, theorem references, resolver and replay boundaries, consumer gates, workload blockers, and explicit non-claims in the reader chapter.
records.companion_treatment: Use the drafting companion note for e-reader and audio review; it gives a short glossary for proof receipt, theorem-linked receipt, resolver-checked, consumer-gated, workload-blocked, fingerprint, replay, and theorem laundering.
records.audio_treatment: Summarize the receipt lifecycle in narration and route the state vocabulary to companion notes rather than reading field lists verbatim.
dense_material: theorem reference, resolver, replay, fingerprint, and consumer-gate vocabulary
must_remain_in_reader: proof authority is not model-quality authority
workload, baseline, metric, and evidence artifacts are still required before downstream quality or runtime promotion
unreplayed or stale theorem references stay residual rather than becoming proof evidence
companion_note_material: short example of a structural receipt that blocks a quality claim
audio note explaining why proof vocabulary is retained but not treated as capability evidence
non_claims: This route does not claim local replay of external Circle proofs.
This route does not promote any model-quality, runtime, memory, context, safety, or ASI capability claim.

records.title: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope
records.reader_treatment: Keep the distinction between Lean predicates, schemas, process validators, behavior tests, benchmarks, external theorem references, semantic adequacy review, and research backlog in the reader chapter.
records.companion_treatment: Add a future companion note that maps proof lanes to ordinary reader language and explains why each lane has a different authority boundary.
records.audio_treatment: Narrate the proof envelope as a claims-control discipline and route detailed lane vocabulary to companion notes or a glossary entry.
must_remain_in_reader: a passed Lean build supports only the finite predicate it checks
schemas, tests, benchmarks, and theorem references are different evidence lanes
semantic adequacy remains required before proof artifacts can strengthen broader prose claims
companion_note_material: proof-lane glossary for Lean, schema, process validator, behavior test, benchmark, external theorem reference, and semantic adequacy
short example showing a valid finite predicate that is too narrow for a broad safety claim
audio note for when proof details are summarized instead of read verbatim
non_claims: This route does not claim proof adequacy beyond the recorded finite predicates.
This route does not promote any chapter claim above architectural argument.

records.title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.reader_treatment: Keep charter, work contract, contribution ledger, treasury policy, event taint, steward action, sunset, worker federation, project-economy, and non-ownership boundaries in the reader chapter for v1.0.
records.companion_treatment: Add a future companion note for the implementation ladder and project-object quick reference; revisit curated reader compression during release editing if the chapter still feels too long.
records.audio_treatment: Summarize project objects and the implementation ladder in narration, with a companion note for exact record names and lifecycle order.
dense_material: worker federation and artifact economy ladder
must_remain_in_reader: the steward is not the owner
authority, spending, governance, and release actions remain bounded by explicit records and human approval
event taint and sunset policy are governance boundaries, not optional implementation details
companion_note_material: implementation ladder summary for e-reader and audio users
future curated-reader compression notes if release editing decides overlays are too broad
non_claims: This route does not create a curated reader chapter.
This route does not claim steward workflow execution, treasury execution, governance correctness, or release readiness.
This routing manifest is not a reader release record.
This routing manifest is not an EPUB, PDF, DOCX, HTML, audio, or audio-embedded EPUB artifact record.
This routing manifest does not move meaning-critical uncertainty out of the reader spine.
This routing manifest does not promote any claim support state.
